observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

Regularly reconcile the cashbook balances with the subsidiary ledgers to ensure accuracy of the cash accounts;

Prepare and timely submit bank reconciliation statements for all accounts maintained by the Municipality and ensure that balances reflected in the BRS are reconciled with the balances in the subsidiary ledger; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to analyze the Trust Fund bank account with a negative balance and determine and effect the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries to arrive at the correct balance of the account.
observations.reason: This was not acted by management. Reiterated as Finding No. 1 in this year’s Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to record in the appropriate Inventory account all purchases of materials and supplies upon acceptance of the delivered items and only recognize the expense upon consumption or distribution;

Direct the General Services Officer to ensure that all issuances are supported with Requisition and Issue Slip which shall be summarized in the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued and shall be submitted the same to the Municipal Accountant for the recording of the corresponding expense; and

Observe proper recording of transactions as prescribed under Volume III of Government Accounting Manual for Local Government Units in order to provide a fair financial statement presentation.
observations.reason: This was not acted by management. Reiterated as Finding No. 3 in this year’s Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above observations, we recommended the following:

Direct the inventory/property committee to conduct an inventory of all the properties of the Municipality and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) with the corresponding remarks on the condition of the listed equipment; 

Reconcile the result thereof with available accounting records to determine unrecorded properties; and

Submit a report on the result of reconciliation which shall be our basis for appropriate opinion and further action.
observations.reason: The management only conducted a partial physical count of PPE. There was no reconciliation of records conducted and that the report submitted by the management did not conform to the prescribed form for RPCPPE. Reiterated as Finding No. 4 in this year’s Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Accounting and Property Section should exert extra effort in observing procedures in the accounting and recording of PPEs as required in Section 41 and 56 of the NGAS Manual for a reliable and informative financial statement.  The following steps have to be undertaken:

Accounting Section:

Determine cut-off period on transaction where data is no longer available for possible requests for authority to write-off as prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-001.

Monitor submission of the Physical Count of PPEs and periodically conduct reconciliation for immediate correction in the books of accounts;

Property Section:

Submit the Inventory report required in Section 56 of the NGAS Manual.

Properly accomplish the RPCPPE. Specifically, the following information and presentation has to be included:

Unit Value and Balance per Card Classification of items/articles shall be presented and classified based on account title.

Obsolete, unserviceable and article not in use should not be included but in a separate Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP).

Recommendations:
We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accounting Office personnel to provide adjustments in the books of accounts regarding the improperly recorded cash advance transactions for official travel and to refer to the Chart of Accounts provided in COA Circular	No. 2015 – 009 for LGUs for reference and guidance in the recording of the financial transactions of the LGU.
Management’s Comments:
The Management acknowledged the audit finding and assured the Audit Team that adjustments will be recorded in CY 2022. They also committed to adhere to the audit recommendations given.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will continue to monitor the implementation of the aforesaid recommendations in the ensuing year.

Recommendations:
We recommended Management to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to record in the appropriate Inventory accounts all purchases of materials and supplies upon acceptance of the delivered items and only recognize the expenses upon consumption or distribution;
Direct the General Services Officer to ensure that all issuances are supported with Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) which shall be summarized in the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and shall then be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for the recording of the corresponding expense; and
Observe proper recording of transactions by referring to the Revised Chart of Accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 to provide a fair financial statement presentation.
Management’s Comments:
The Management was amenable to the audit finding and committed to implementing the audit recommendations.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will continue to monitor the implementation of the aforesaid recommendations in the ensuing year.

Recommendations:
We recommended Management to:
Direct the GASO and the MAO to establish an effective and practical system that would ensure the swift and regular reconciliation of records of both offices pertaining to the Municipality’s Property, Plant and Equipment;
Conduct a complete physical count of all properties;
Implement a system of property management such as using property tags and assigning property numbers for each of the property own to facilitate easy identification; and
Instruct the GASO to correctly use the appropriate form, which is the RPCPPE, and provide all the necessary data required for proper identification of all PPE items.
Management’s Comments:
The Management acknowledged the audit findings and assured the Audit Team that the audit recommendations will be implemented.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will continue to monitor the implementation of the aforementioned recommendations in the ensuing year.

Recommendations:
We recommended Management to:
1. Enforce the immediate liquidation and/or settlement of all outstanding cash advances;
2. Stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to officials/employees who have previous unliquidated cash advances as well as granting multiple cash advances to the same employees regardless of the purpose;
3. Direct the Municipal Accountant to expedite the request for the write-off of long outstanding cash advances that are considered dormant in accordance with the guidelines and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and
4. Strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, in the granting and liquidation of cash advances
Management’s Comments:
The Management affirmed the audit findings and informed the Audit Team that demand letters were already issued to the officials/employees concerned. The Accountant further explained that the bulk of the said cash advances were dormant cash advances which will be subjected to requests for write-off and unliquidated confidential/intelligence funds of the current and prior administrations. The request for write-off shall be filed after the completion of the documentary requirements needed. The management committed to implementing the audit recommendations.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team acknowledged the prompt action done by Management and further reiterated that Management must strictly comply with the provisions in the granting and liquidation of cash advances as prescribed COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 in CY 2022 onwards.

Recommendations:
We recommended Management to:
Direct the Municipal Agriculture Office and the Municipal Planning and Development Office to coordinate with and assist the Municipal Accountant to demand from the NGOs/POs to submit the documentary requirements to facilitate the recording of the liquidation of funds transferred;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to exert efforts for the liquidation of “Due from NGOs/POs” account to avoid dormancy; and
Exhaust all legal efforts to demand from the concerned NGOs/POs to produce and submit the liquidation reports and supporting documents of the utilized funds transferred to their respective organizations.
Management’s Comments:
The Management was amenable to the audit finding and committed to implementing the audit recommendations. They informed the Audit Team that they had coordinated with the focal persons of the two (2) NGOs concerned and had demanded from them the submission of the NGO’s file copy of the liquidation reports. However, none were retained by the NGOs as all documents were allegedly submitted to the former Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer. The Management also informed the Audit Team that as per verification, both projects were successfully implemented.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will continue to monitor the implementation of the aforesaid recommendations in the ensuing year.

Recommendations:

1. Submit immediately the lacking relief distribution sheets or any form of acknowledgment evidencing that the assistance was duly received by the intended recipients; and
2. Ensure that subsequent transactions pertaining to the distribution of any form of assistance must be supported with complete documentation.
Management’s Comments:
The Management was amenable to the audit observation and assured the Audit Team that the distribution list will be submitted immediately and the above audit recommendations will be adhered to.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will continue to monitor the implementation of the aforesaid recommendations in the ensuing year.

Recommendations:
We recommended that management:
1. Direct Municipal Accountant to look into the details of the unspent balances pertaining to fund transfers taken up as liability in the General Fund and Trust Fund books and facilitate the return to the source agency or remittance to the National Treasury or transfer to the General Fund of the Agency, whichever is appropriate, and make to the necessary adjustments in the books immediately;
2. Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office to coordinate with and assist the Municipal Accountant in verifying whether the projects/programs associated with the funds received were already implemented and to secure the necessary documents to facilitate the recording of adjustments in the books; and
3. Ensure proper coordination between the Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) and the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) in the future project/program implementation toward the funds which may be received from NGAs.
Management’s Comments:
The Management acknowledged the audit observation and assured the Audit Team that the Accountant will look into the details of these balances and will coordinate with the source agencies to ascertain the proper action that must be taken. The Management committed to implement the audit recommendations.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will continue to monitor the implementation of the aforesaid recommendations in the ensuing year.

Recommendations:
We recommended Management to:
Adequately plan and thoroughly study the feasibility of each project before proposing it to be included as one of the projects to be funded by the 20% LGDF;
Ensure immediate action and exert additional effort for the timely implementation of the projects; and
Ensure the full utilization of the appropriated amounts for the benefit of the constituents for PPAs to be implemented under 20% LGDF.
Management’s Comments:
The Management was amenable to the audit finding and explained that the lapses were due to the pandemic constraints and the lack of manpower in the Municipal Engineering Office to undertake all the planned projects in CY 2021. The Management committed to implementing the audit recommendations.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will continue to monitor the implementation of the aforesaid recommendations in the ensuing year.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management strictly follow the guidelines spelled out in the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of RA 9184 and submit documents or justification about why there was no mode of procurement adopted for the above-discussed transactions for the audit team to decide in audit.

The Management was amenable to the audit observation and explained that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) was not aware that BAC resolutions must be submitted to the Accounting Office and the Office of the Auditor. The Management had submitted the BAC Resolutions which the Audit Team will examine and further evaluate. The Audit Team will also continue to monitor the implementation of the aforesaid recommendations in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Accounting and Property Section should exert extra effort in observing procedures in the accounting and recording of PPEs as required in Section 41 and 56 of the NGAS Manual for a reliable and informative financial statement.  The following steps have to be undertaken:

Accounting Section:

Maintain PPELC for each ledger accounts under the PPE. Start from the current year and backwards.

Determine cut-off period on transaction where data is no longer available for possible requests for authority to write-off as prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-001.

Monitor submission of the Physical Count of PPEs and periodically conduct reconciliation for immediate correction in the books of accounts;

Recording unserviceable properties reported in the IIRUP in Other Assets and effect adjustment in the PPE.

Property Section:

Prepare timetable for the conduct of Physical Inventory and coordinate with the Inventory Committee members for a cohesive planning

Update recording of Property Cards to facilitate preparation of the Inventory report to be presented per category of PPEs;

Submit the Inventory report required in Section 56 of the NGAS Manual.

Properly accomplish the RPCPPE. Specifically, the following information and presentation has to be included:

Unit Value and Balance per Card Classification of items/ articles shall be presented and classified based on account title.

Obsolete, unserviceable and article not in use should not be included but in a separate Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended  management  to  direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
prepare and submit on time the monthly  bank  reconciliation  statements  in
all bank accounts maintained by the Municipality.

We recommended that the correct  amount  of  RPT  and  SET  Receivable
should be established and recorded at the beginning of  the  year  based  on
the Municipal Treasurer’s certified list showing the name of the taxpayers.

In view of the above observations, we recommended the following:

   1. Direct the inventory/property committee to conduct an inventory of all
      the properties of the Municipality  and  prepare  the  Report  on  the
      Physical Count of Property, Plant  and  Equipment  (RPCPPE)  with  the
      corresponding remarks on the condition of the listed equipment;


   2. Reconcile the result thereof  with  available  accounting  records  to
      determine unrecorded properties; and


   3. Submit a report on the result of reconciliation  which  shall  be  our
      basis for appropriate opinion and further action.

We recommended the following:

   a. Stop immediately  the  present  system  in  handling  PCF  and  ensure
      compliance with the NGAS for LGUs in maintaining the  same  under  the
      imprest system;

   b. Direct the Municipal  Accountant/PCF  Custodian  to  ensure  that  all
      disbursements  under  the  PCV  should  be  supported  with   complete
      supporting documents to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit;

   c. Appoint permanent and bonded personnel in handling the PCF;

   d. Use the PCF only for those activities requiring items of  small  value
      when it is inconvenient to pay through check; and

   e. Adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 in procuring regular  expenses  of
      the Municipality.

We recommended management to direct the Municipal  Treasurer  to  stop
the practice of using cash collections and to demand payment/liquidation  to
personnel or officials who have outstanding vales out of the collection  and
to remit immediately the collection intact.

We recommended management to direct the Budget Office  to  justify  in
writing the excessive appropriation and consider the schedule and timing  of
payment for loan amortization in allocating funds intended  for  paying  off
LGU debts in succeeding budget year.

We recommended Management to record fund transfers to  barangay  under
the account code 1-03-03-030 – Due from Local  Government  Units  to  ensure
proper monitoring of fund releases and  the  corresponding  amount  utilized
for the approved purpose; and enforce liquidation of  the  fund  transferred
to these barangays to  ensure  that  this  was  utilized  for  the  intended
projects or the same shall be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct
a  thorough  evaluation/reconciliation  of  the  journal  entries  made   to
determine the causes of the  discrepancy.  Likewise,  effect  the  necessary
adjustments to clear the reciprocal accounts of possible  erroneous  entries
and/or omissions made.

We recommended the following:


     1. Direct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort for the liquidation
        of Due from NGOs/POs account in order to avoid dormancy and closely
        monitor and regularly check the implementation of the projects;


     2. Exhaust all legal efforts to demand from the concerned NGOs/POs  to
        produce and submit the liquidation reports and supporting documents
        of   the   utilized   funds   transferred   to   their   respective
        organizations; and


     3. Prepare aging of dormant fund transfers to NGOs/POs on a  quarterly
        basis to  support  request  for  write-off  as  stipulated  in  COA
        Circular 2016-005.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant    shall monitor the utilization of the funds    received from national government agencies and    immediately cause the return of the unutilized    balance to the Source Agency after the    completion of every project.

We also recommended that the LGU make    representation to the DA-RO XI so that the    request for amendments of units and unit prices    will be favorably acted upon in order to avoid    possible penalties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that preparation of the    Annual Investment Plan should be in compliance    with the provisions enumerated in DBM-DILG    Joint Circular Memorandum 2011-1 and ensure that    utilization of the 20% Development Fund should be in    accordance with the plan.    We also recommended to stop the practice of    utilizing 20% Development Fund to other purposes for which    the fund was created.    We further recommended to direct the Municipal    Development Council to provide detailed descriptions and/or    project designs for all the PPAs in the AIP to    determine if such were attributed for development    purposes.

Most of these officers have been reassigned from one office to another that contributed to the reliability of the said reports.

We further recommend that    preparation of the Annual Investment Plan should be compliant with    the provisions enumerated in DBM-DILG Joint Circular    Memorandum 2011-1 and ensure that utilization of the same is in    accordance with the plan. With this, social, economic and    environmental development would be achieved by the    Municipality.

We recommended that management to monitor    and scrutinize the transactions charged to SEF to prevent future    suspensions and disallowance in audit.    In addition, we recommended that Local School    Board prepare the budget for SEF according to the rules and    regulations of RA 5447 so that funds will be spent for the intended    purpose to the benefit of the students and teacher alike.

We further recommended the Management to allocate a separate    budget of LDRRMC for the expenditures that does not fall within the    aforementioned guidelines to avoid probable suspensions and    disallowance.

Furnish the Audit Team with the list of accountable officers    with unliquidated and outstanding cash advances, together with    copy of written notices duly acknowledged by the    concerned accountable officers. observations.recommendation: We have recommended that responsible officials of    the LGU-Banaybanay should strictly observe the    guidelines prescribed in Section in Section 3 of the DILG &    DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2013.    In order to be responsive to the needs of the constituents, a    tangible development projects that partake of capital investments that    would directly contribute to the increased income earning    capacity of the LGU or citizen such as increased employment    opportunities in the locality, improved additional facilities    for industry, investment and trades, transportation,    communication and improvement of sites and services; and    environmental protection and management must be given priority in the    planning and programming of activities for the utilization of the said    fund, otherwise all other expenses not directly related thereto    shall be included in other appropriate budgetary allocations.

We have recommended that the Municipal General    Services Office (MGSO) or Officials involved in the    procurement process to review documents before approval by the    higher authorities and strictly follow the provisions of COA    Circular No. 2009-001 by submitting to COA copies of contracts and POs    within five (5) days after its issuance or perfection to facilitate    evaluation and validation of transactions.    Require the MGSO or the personnel concerned to provide all the needed    information in the purchase order to ensure prompt and accurate    delivery of goods purchased and to avoid legal impediments.

Likewise, monitoring and    evaluation on the reported accomplishment reports shall be    conducted to come up with a more responsive GAD Plan.  Moreover,    direct the Budget Officer to allocate no less than five percent    (5%) of the total appropriations of the General Fund for the    implementation of GAD Plan.

We have recommended that the General Services    Officer and the Motorpool in-charge should comply strictly    with the provisions of Section 4.6 of PD 1445 and COA    Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Require the submission of the required reports as enumerated in COA    Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We have recommended that the Accounting and Property Section should exert extra effort in observing procedures in the accounting and recording of PPEs as required in Section 41 and 56 of the NGAS Manual for a reliable and informative financial statement. The following steps have to be undertaken: Accounting Section: Maintain PPELC for each ledger accounts under the PPE. Start from the current year and backwards. Determine cut-off period on transaction where data is no longer available for possible requests for authority to write-off as prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-001. Monitor submission of the Physical Count of PPEs and periodically conduct reconciliation for immediate correction in the books of accounts; Recording unserviceable properties reported in the IIRUP in Other Assets and effect adjustment in the PPE. Property Section: Prepare timetable for the conduct of Physical Inventory and coordinate with the Inventory Committee members for a cohesive planning Update recording of Property Cards to facilitate preparation of the Inventory report to be presented per category of PPEs; Submit the Inventory report required in Section 56 of the NGAS Manual. Properly accomplish the RPCPPE. Specifically, the following information and presentation has to be included: Unit Value and Balance per Card Classification of items/articles shall be presented and classified based on account title. Obsolete, unserviceable and article not in use should not be included but in a separate Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP).

It is recommended that the accounting and the General Services Officers should adopt systems and procedures required in the accounting and recording of PPEs for a fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements. The Accounting Section shall: Maintain PPELC for each ledger accounts under the PPE as required in Sec. 114 of NGAS manual. Start from the current year and backwards. Determine cut-off period for transactions where data is no longer available for possible application of the request for write-off prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-001. Monitor submission of the Physical Count of PPE and periodically conduct reconciliation of accounts for immediate preparation of JEV on accounts requiring adjustments. The General Services office should prepare specific timetable for the conduct of physical inventory and preparation of the corresponding report for a timely submission on or before January 31 of each year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should comply with the
   documentary requirements provided under Sec. 3 of COA Circular No.  2012-
   001 to ensure the propriety and validity of the transaction and to record
   fund transfers to barangay under the account code 1-03-03-030 – Due  from
   Local Government Units to ensure proper monitoring of fund releases.


        We  further  recommended  to  enforce  liquidation  of   the   fund
   transferred to the barangays to ensure that this  was  utilized  for  the
   intended projects.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Accountant  shall  monitor  the
   utilization of the funds received from national government  agencies  and
   immediately cause the return of the  unutilized  balance  to  the  Source
   Agency after the completion of every project.

We recommended that the Bids  and  Awards  Committee  expedite  the
   implementation of the corresponding procurement activities and BUB  Focal
   Persons to fast track the preparation of program of  works  in  order  to
   implement the intended projects.


        We also recommended that the LGU make representation to  the  DA-RO
   XI so that the request for amendments of units and unit  prices  will  be
   favorably acted upon in order to avoid possible penalties.

We recommended that  preparation  of the  Annual  Investment  Plan
   should be in compliance with the provisions enumerated in DBM-DILG  Joint
   Circular Memorandum  2011-1  and  ensure  that  utilization  of  the  20%
   Development Fund should be in accordance with the plan.


        We  also  recommended  to  stop  the  practice  of  utilizing   20%
   Development Fund to other purposes for which the fund was created.


        We further recommended to direct the Municipal Development  Council
   to provide detailed descriptions and/or project designs for all the  PPAs
   in the AIP to determine if such were attributed for development purposes.

We recommended the following courses of action:

   a. Action  Plan  should  be  prepared  indicating  therein  the  specific
      programs and activities to be funded and implemented out of this fund.
   b. Allocate funds from the General Fund for the  administrative  expenses
      of the MDRRM Office and ensure charges to the LDRRM Fund  are  limited
      only to programs and activities in support of disaster risk  reduction
      and management.

We recommended that management should avoid charging  expenses  not
   within the priority expenses as enumerated under DECS, DBM and DILG Joint
   Circular No. 01 and to advise the Local School Board to effectively  plan
   for the specific programs, projects and activities which strictly  adhere
   to the provisions of the said circular.

We  recommended  that  Management  should  submit  immediately  the
   lacking supporting documents so that the audit team  can  conduct  proper
   evaluation on the legality of the transactions.



        We   further   recommended   that   management   provides   written
   explanations and justifications on the following:


        a. Why was the procurement of seven units heavy equipment  and  one
           unit coaster bus was divided into three lots?
        b. Why was the deliveries  of  these  equipment  were  done  in  an
           installment basis and took more than a year?
c. Why was the loaned amount for Lot 3 exceeded the bid amount of JROG?
        d. Why some of the resolutions and minutes had no signatures of the
           concerned official/personnel, as shown in Appendix II.B.1?
        e. Why did the schedule of activities not adhere in accordance with
           BAC Resolution No. B-10-2014 (see Appendix II.G.2)?
        f. Why many of the BAC resolutions did not have complete signatures
           of the concerned personnel?
        g. Why the Notice of Award did not indicate the dates of receipt by
           JROG Marketing?

We recommended that RPT and SET Receivable should be set up at  the
   beginning of the year based on the Municipal Treasurer’s  certified  list
   showing the name of the taxpayers.


        We also recommended that Municipal Accountant should refer to Sec.
   36 Volume 1 of the Manual on the New  Government  Accounting  System  for
   LGUs for the proper entries in setting up the RPT  and  SET  Receivables,
   and to  strictly  follow  the  proper  processes  on  the  reporting  and
   recording of RPT and SET to fairly present the balances in the  financial
   statements.

We recommended that management should  only  grant  cash  advances  to
   bonded permanent employees and enforce  liquidation  within  the  prescribed
   period. In addition, the Municipal Accountant should  require  a  designated
   accountable officer to liquidate all outstanding cash  advances  granted  to
   him/her  before  the  approval  of  additional   cash   advance   to   avoid
   accumulation of the same at year-end.

      We also recommended that management should have proper planning  (such
   as proper preparation of Annual Procurement Plan) that includes  identifying
   all activities that needs to be funded for a specified  period  to  minimize
   granting of cash advances.

We have recommended that the offices concerned to comply and submit within the prescribed period the submission of the above mentioned financial and other reports/records; otherwise, the penal provision under section 122 of PD No. 1445 shall be applied.

The Bids and Awards Committee should also strictly implement the IRR of RA 9184 on the procurement to avoid any suspension and disallowance.

Liquidate the cash advance for local travel within 30 days after return to his official station or refund the amount. We recommended the Municipal Mayor to cause the refund of honoraria received by aforementioned personnel and discontinue the granting thereof.

We recommended that payment of honoraria shall be limited within the amount collected from the sale of bid documents and/or savings from General Fund as allowed under DBM Circular 2007-3 to avoid suspensions and disallowance in audit. Likewise, please submit a proof for a successfully completed procurement project monthly to enable us to determine the correct amount of honoraria to be received by each concerned personnel.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the aforementioned lacking documents to the Office of the Auditor to enable this office to verify the regularity and validity of the payments. We further recommended the Municipal Accountant to exercise prudence and diligence in exercising his duties and responsibilities being accountable and responsible person in examining voluminous transactions of the agency before payments are made.

We recommended that Management should limit the hiring of job order to the actual needs of the concerned offices and to require the Human Resource and Management Office (HRMO) to coordinate with the Heads of Division/Office to prepare a well-defined job description of every JO stating therein the specific duties and responsibilities to ensure that the services rendered is necessary in the agency’s mandate and program objectives.

We recommended that management to monitor and scrutinize the transactions charged to SEF to prevent future suspensions and disallowance in audit. In addition, we recommended that Local School Board prepare the budget for SEF according to the rules and regulations of RA 5447 so that funds will be spent for the intended purpose to the benefit of the students and teacher alike.

We recommended the MDRRM action officer to exercise prudence and diligence in the disbursement of the LDRRMF and to strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 10121 and COA Circular 2012-002 to carry out the objectives of the laws for immediate protection of the populace. We further recommended the Management to allocate a separate budget of LDRRMC for the expenditures that does not fall within the aforementioned guidelines to avoid probable suspensions and disallowance.

Require the Municipal Accountant to check the propriety and validity of the claims for honorarium to Bids and Awards Committee through strict implementation of Section 5.1 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A & Section 5.7.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Payments for BAC honorarium should be supported with proper and complete documents to prove the propriety of payments.

Require the Liquidating Officer and Municipal Treasurer to monitor closely the turnover of collections by collectors and prompt deposit of said collections by the Liquidating Officer through strict enforcement of the provisions of Sections 60A, 60B and 60C of LTOM and Section 26 and 27 of COA Circular 92-382.

We have recommended that Management should implement policies and control procedures in the granting and liquidation of cash advances through the following: Stop the granting of cash advance to official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him/her is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made in compliance with the above-referred provisions of PD1445; Regularly send confirmation/demand letters as basis in the reconciliation of accounting and accountable officers record and as basis in imposing disciplinary measures; Strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2012-004 for long outstanding accounts. Furnish the Audit Team with the list of accountable officers with unliquidated and outstanding cash advances, together with copy of written notices duly acknowledged by the concerned accountable officers. Submit also copies of disbursement vouchers (DVs) and related supporting documents of those with outstanding balances, especially long overdue accounts.

We have recommended that Management should strictly adhere to the basic principle of paying claims in the form of checks. Limit the granting of cash advances for field operations/activity only or in some activities wherein impractical to pay in check. Moreover, procurement of commodities/goods, ordinary and regular office supplies and equipment needed in the agency’s mandate and nature of operations should form part of the quarterly supply and material requirement of the office as programmed. Follow the procurement processes as prescribed in the Revised IRR of RA 9184. Liquidation Report shall be supported with complete documentation similar to transactions paid in the form of checks.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall require the Head of the MDRRMC to be prudent in the disbursement of the LDRRMF and shall limit its expenditures in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 and DILG Circular 2012-73 in order to faithfully carry out the objectives of the fund, for the immediate protection of the environment and the inhabitants in times of calamity.

We have recommended that responsible officials of the LGU-Banaybanay should strictly observe the guidelines prescribed in Section in Section 3 of the DILG & DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2013. In order to be responsive to the needs of the constituents, a tangible development projects that partake of capital investments that would directly contribute to the increased income earning capacity of the LGU or citizen such as increased employment opportunities in the locality, improved additional facilities for industry, investment and trades, transportation, communication and improvement of sites and services; and environmental protection and management must be given priority in the planning and programming of activities for the utilization of the said fund, otherwise all other expenses not directly related thereto shall be included in other appropriate budgetary allocations.

We have recommended that the Municipal General Services Office (MGSO) or Officials involved in the procurement process to review documents before approval by the higher authorities and strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 by submitting to COA copies of contracts and POs within five (5) days after its issuance or perfection to facilitate evaluation and validation of transactions. Require the MGSO or the personnel concerned to provide all the needed information in the purchase order to ensure prompt and accurate delivery of goods purchased and to avoid legal impediments.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Gender and Development (GAD) Focal Person & Municipal Officers concerned to develop specific programs designed for the gender concerns.  Likewise, monitoring and evaluation on the reported accomplishment reports shall be conducted to come up with a more responsive GAD Plan.  Moreover, direct the Budget Officer to allocate no less than five percent (5%) of the total appropriations of the General Fund for the implementation of GAD Plan.  We recommended further that the Local Chief Executive shall require the GAD Focal Person to be prudent in the utilization of Gender and Development Fund and shall limit its expenditures to its related activities only and in order to faithfully carry out the objectives of the fund, alleviate the poverty and economic empowerment among woman and the protection, promotion, and fulfillment of women’s practice of gender-responsive governance.  We recommended further that the Local Chief Executive shall require the GAD Focal Person to be prudent in the utilization of Gender and Development Fund and shall limit its expenditures to its related activities only and in order to faithfully carry out the objectives of the fund, alleviate the poverty and economic empowerment among woman and the protection, promotion, and fulfillment of women’s practice of gender-responsive governance.  We recommended further that the Local Chief Executive shall require the GAD Focal Person to be prudent

We have recommended that the General Services Officer and the Motorpool in-charge should comply strictly with the provisions of Section 4.6 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.  A preliminary inspection on the motor vehicle subjected for repair should be conducted to determine its physical condition, the extent of damage and repair needed and subsequently conduct post inspection after the repair to ensure that the repair has been undertaken in accordance with the plan and scope of work.  Require the submission of the required reports as enumerated in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We have recommended that Management should clearly monitor the use of government motor vehicles which should be limited to official purposes only through the proper accomplishment of Driver’s Trip Tickets on a daily basis. Likewise, compliance of a Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption on official travels made is enjoined.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall require the Head of the MDRRMC to be prudent in the disbursement of the LDRRMF and shall limit its expenditures to those prescribed in the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 and DILG Circular 2012-73 in order to faithfully carry out the objectives of the fund, for the immediate protection of the environment and the inhabitants in times of calamity.
observations.reason: Inadequate monitoring of LDRRMF and its utilization.

observations.recommendation: Adhere strictly with the provisions of RA 9184 adopting public bidding as the general mode of procurement. For this purpose, the agency shall see to it that the procurement program allows sufficient lead time for such public bidding. Alternative methods should be resorted to only in the highly exceptional cases provided for in Rule XVI of IRR-A of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Management limits procurement on reimbursement basis on exceptionally urgent items.
observations.reason: The LGU still practice procurement of goods and services thru re-imbursement s.

observations.recommendation: Create a committee to conduct an evaluation on the defects found on the Construction of the Municipal Port, and submit a report thereon to COA to avert the Notice of Suspension maturing into Notice of Disallowance. Appropriate an amount for the immediate repair on the defects of the construction of Municipal Port to make it operational and generate more income to meet its monthly loan amortization.
observations.managementAction: The LCE issued EO 6, s.2010 in April 2010 creating Tech. Inspection Committee to conduct evaluation on defects found on the const. of Municipal Port, and the same month the Committee reported reasons for scaling and cracks that appeared on the concrete pavement of the structure was due to movements of the container vans installed underneath water at the port which was brought about by strong sea current and earth movements. Repair and reconstruction went underway until end of CY 2010. Certificate of completion dated Jan.25, 2011 was submitted to the Team on Feb. 10, 2011.
